387	/-- The pure pullback **pentagon** at `F`, in transport-light form: the content of
388	`pushPullMap_comp` once the units and pushforwards are peeled. It is exactly
389	`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := b) (g := a) (h := p₁)` evaluated at
390	`F`, with the associator (an identity on components) absorbed and the two leading
391	comparison isos inverted. Project-local. -/
392	lemma pushPull_pentagon {Z₁ Z₂ Z₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (b : Z₃ ⟶ Z₂)
393	    (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X) (F : X.Modules) :
394	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
395	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F ≫
396	        eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
397	          (Category.assoc b a p₁)) =
398	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
399	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F := by
400	  have H := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := b) (g := a) (h := p₁)
401	  have HF := congrArg (fun t => NatTrans.app t F) H
402	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
403	    Functor.associator_hom_app, eqToHom_app, Functor.comp_obj,
404	    Category.id_comp] at HF
405	  -- HF : A1⁻¹ ≫ B1⁻¹ ≫ C ≫ D = eqToHom eF, with the associator (= 𝟙) absorbed.
406	  -- Cancel the two leading isos `B1 = (pullback b).map (pullbackComp a p₁).hom`, `A1 = pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)`
407	  -- against their inverses (no `IsIso` instances needed: `Iso.hom_inv_id_app` + functoriality),
408	  -- then feed `HF` in via `congrArg`/`trans` (defeq, so no fragile `rw [HF]` matching).
409	  have cancel : (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
410	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F ≫
411	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).inv.app F ≫
412	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).inv.app F) ≫
413	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
414	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F =
415	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
416	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F := by
417	    have h1 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F ≫
418	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).inv.app F = 𝟙 _ := Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _
419	    have h2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
420	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).inv.app F) = 𝟙 _ :=
421	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
422	        ((congrArg (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _)).trans
423	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map_id _))
424	    simp only [Functor.comp_obj] at h1 h2 ⊢
425	    rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]
426	  have hcd := cancel.symm.trans (congrArg (fun t => (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map
427	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
428	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F ≫ t) HF)
429	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app
430	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F))
431	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F), hcd]
432	  simp [eqToHom_trans]

435	/-- Composition law for `rawPushPullMap` with the two over-triangles as free
436	hypotheses (kernel-cheap). -/
437	lemma rawPushPullMap_comp {Z₁ Z₂ Z₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (b : Z₃ ⟶ Z₂)
438	    (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X) (p₂ : Z₂ ⟶ X) (p₃ : Z₃ ⟶ X)
439	    (wg : a ≫ p₁ = p₂) (wh : b ≫ p₂ = p₃) (F : X.Modules) :
440	    rawPushPullMap (b ≫ a) p₁ p₃ (by rw [Category.assoc, wg, wh]) F =
441	      rawPushPullMap a p₁ p₂ wg F ≫ rawPushPullMap b p₂ p₃ wh F := by
442	  subst wg wh
443	  rw [rawPushPullMap_self a p₁ F, rawPushPullMap_self b (a ≫ p₁) F,
444	      rawPushPullMap_self_gen (b ≫ a) p₁ (b ≫ a ≫ p₁) (Category.assoc b a p₁) F]
445	  -- The over-triangle `eqToHom` (in `X.Modules`) is `(pushforward p₁).map` of the
446	  -- corresponding `Z₁.Modules`-level `eqToHom` (`pushforward` is strict).
447	  have he : eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
448	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.assoc b a p₁)) =
449	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map (eqToHom (congrArg
450	        (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (b ≫ a)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F))
451	        (Category.assoc b a p₁))) := by
452	    rw [eqToHom_map]; rfl
453	  -- The inner identity in `Z₁.Modules`: the pure pushforward-of-pentagon content.
454	  have INNER : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (b ≫ a)).unit.app
455	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
456	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (b ≫ a)).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F) ≫
457	        eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (b ≫ a)).obj
458	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.assoc b a p₁)) =
459	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
460	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
461	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) ≫
462	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
463	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
464	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (a ≫ p₁)).obj F) ≫
465	            (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
466	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F)) := by
467	    rw [pushPull_unit_comp b a ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)]
468	    -- The `Z₂.Modules`-level content: the pushforward-`b` of the pullback pentagon, with the
469	    -- composite unit straightened by naturality of `η^b`.
470	    have INNER2 :
471	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
472	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫
473	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
474	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
475	              (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F ≫
476	              eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
477	                (Category.assoc b a p₁))) =
478	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F ≫
479	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
480	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (a ≫ p₁)).obj F) ≫
481	            (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
482	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F) := by
483	      have key2 : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
484	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
485	              (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F ≫
486	              eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
487	                (Category.assoc b a p₁))) =
488	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
489	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
490	              (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F) :=
491	        congrArg _ (pushPull_pentagon a b p₁ F)
492	      have nat2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
493	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫
494	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
495	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) =
496	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F ≫
497	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
498	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (a ≫ p₁)).obj F) :=
499	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.naturality
500	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)).symm
501	      refine (congrArg (fun t => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
502	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫ t) key2).trans ?_
503	      rw [Functor.map_comp]
504	      exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
505	        ((congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
506	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F)) nat2).trans (Category.assoc _ _ _))
507	    exact congrArg (fun t => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
508	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map t) INNER2
509	  -- Expose the second RHS factor as `(pushforward p₁).map (…)` (strictness, by `rfl`) so the
510	  -- `map_comp` unifications below stay kernel-cheap.
511	  rw [show (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ p₁)).map
512	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
513	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (a ≫ p₁)).obj F) ≫
514	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F)) =
515	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
516	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app
517	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (a ≫ p₁)).obj F) ≫
518	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F)))
519	        from rfl]
520	  convert congrArg (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map INNER using 2

522	/-- **Push–pull functor `G` — composition law** (contravariant functoriality).
523	For composable morphisms `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁`, `h : Y₃ ⟶ Y₂` of `X`-schemes,
524	`G(h ≫ g) = G(g) ≫ G(h)` (writing `≫` for `Over X`-composition; in the informal
525	`∘`-notation of the blueprint this is `G(g ∘ h) = G(h) ∘ G(g)`). Together with
526	`pushPullMap_id` this assembles `pushPullObj`/`pushPullMap` into a genuine functor
527	`(Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. Project-local (`lem:push_pull_comp`). -/
528	lemma pushPullMap_comp (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ Y₃ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) (h : Y₃ ⟶ Y₂) :
529	    pushPullMap F (h ≫ g) = pushPullMap F g ≫ pushPullMap F h := by
530	  rw [pushPullMap_eq_raw, pushPullMap_eq_raw, pushPullMap_eq_raw]
531	  exact rawPushPullMap_comp g.left h.left Y₁.hom Y₂.hom Y₃.hom (Over.w g) (Over.w h) F
532	
533	/-- **The push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`**, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`.
534	Assembled from the object brick `pushPullObj`, the morphism brick `pushPullMap`, and
535	the two functor laws `pushPullMap_id` / `pushPullMap_comp`. Contravariant on `Over X`
536	(hence a covariant functor out of `(Over X)ᵒᵖ`): a morphism `φ : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂` in
537	`(Over X)ᵒᵖ` is `φ.unop : Y₂.unop ⟶ Y₁.unop` in `Over X`, sent to
538	`pushPullMap F φ.unop`. This is the functor the planner calls `G`; composing the
539	geometric Čech backbone `coverCechNerve` with it produces the cosimplicial
540	`O_X`-module nerve `CechNerve`. Project-local (`lem:push_pull_comp` consumer). -/
541	noncomputable def pushPullFunctor (F : X.Modules) : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules where
542	  obj Y := pushPullObj F Y.unop
543	  map φ := pushPullMap F φ.unop
544	  map_id Y := pushPullMap_id F Y.unop
545	  map_comp φ ψ := pushPullMap_comp F φ.unop ψ.unop
546	
547	/-- The geometric Čech backbone, lifted to a **simplicial object in `Over X`**: each
548	fibre power carries its structure map to `X` (the augmentation of `coverCechNerve`),
549	and the face/degeneracy maps are `X`-morphisms by naturality of the augmentation into
550	the constant functor. Project-local; obtained from `coverCechNerve` by
551	`CategoryTheory.Over.lift` applied to the augmentation natural transformation. -/
552	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOver (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : SimplicialObject (Over X) :=
553	  Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom
554	
555	/-- The over-category Čech backbone as an **augmented** simplicial object in `Over X`:
556	`coverCechNerveOver` augmented by the terminal object `Over.mk (𝟙 X)` of `Over X`. The
557	augmentation map at each simplicial level is the unique morphism to the terminal object
558	(its underlying scheme map is the level's structure map to `X`), and the augmentation
559	coherence condition holds automatically because the augmentation target is terminal.
560	Project-local. -/
561	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOverAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
562	    SimplicialObject.Augmented (Over X) :=
563	  SimplicialObject.augment (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰) (Over.mk (𝟙 X))
564	    (Over.mkIdTerminal.from _)
565	    (fun _ _ _ => Over.mkIdTerminal.hom_ext _ _)
566	
567	/-- The cosimplicial `O_X`-module obtained by post-composing the over-category Čech
568	backbone `coverCechNerveOver` (read contravariantly, via `Functor.leftOp`) with the
569	push–pull functor `pushPullFunctor F = G`. This is the underlying cosimplicial object
570	of `CechNerve` (before adjoining the augmentation `F`). Project-local. -/
571	noncomputable def cechNerveCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
572	    CosimplicialObject X.Modules :=
573	  (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰 : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X).rightOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F
574	
575	/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
576	cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).
577	
578	For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
579	sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules whose
580	object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the `(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of
581	indices, of the direct images `(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of
582	`F` to the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
583	immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that omit one
584	index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree `-1` is `F` itself
585	on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection `U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.
586	
587	This is now *constructed*, not postulated: the geometric backbone `coverCechNerveOverAug`
588	(the augmented Čech nerve of the cover as an augmented simplicial object in `Over X`,
589	unconditional because `Scheme` has all finite limits) is read contravariantly
590	(`SimplicialObject.Augmented.rightOp`) and whiskered by the push–pull functor
591	`pushPullFunctor F = G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. The whiskering
592	transports both the cosimplicial structure and the augmentation, so the augmentation
593	point is `G` applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`, namely
594	`(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`. The functor laws of `G` (`pushPullMap_id`, `pushPullMap_comp`)
595	are what make this assembly legitimate.
596	
597	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
598	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
599	noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
600	    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
601	  (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)).obj
602	    (coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).rightOp
603	
604	/-- **Relative {\v C}ech complex from a cosimplicial nerve** (coherence-free
605	plumbing). Given `f : X ⟶ S` and an augmented cosimplicial object `N` of
606	`O_X`-modules, produce the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` by:
607	forgetting the augmentation (`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop`), pushing the
608	cosimplicial object forward along `f` (`CosimplicialObject.whiskering` applied to
609	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`), and taking the alternating coface-map cochain
610	complex (`alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). This is the entire passage `CechNerve ↦
611	CechComplex`, and it uses no `pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence — only
612	the (pre)additivity of `S.Modules`. Project-local. -/
613	noncomputable def relativeCechComplexOfNerve (f : X ⟶ S)
614	    (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules) : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
615	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S.Modules).obj
616	    (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
617	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N))
618	
619	/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
620	Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).
621	
622	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
623	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
624	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
625	```
626	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
627	```
628	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
629	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
630	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
631	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
632	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
633	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
634	quasi-coherent.
635	
636	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
637	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
638	noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
639	    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
640	  -- Construction (Stacks): apply the relative pushforward `f_*` over each finite
641	  -- intersection to the Čech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, then take the alternating-sum
642	  -- Čech differential. This is exactly the coherence-free plumbing
643	  -- `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, so `CechComplex` is genuinely *defined* in terms
644	  -- of the nerve: an axiom-clean `CechNerve` immediately yields an axiom-clean
645	  -- `CechComplex`. The only remaining hole is `CechNerve` itself.
646	  relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)
647	
648	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the augmented Čech complex on `X`
649	
650	The lemma `cechAugmented_exact` (`lem:cech_augmented_resolution`) asserts that the
651	augmented {\v C}ech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`
652	over an affine open cover `𝒰` is **exact** in `QCoh(X)` — i.e. the {\v C}ech nerve
653	is a resolution of `F`. This is the *un-pushed* (over `X`, not relative to `f`)
654	counterpart of `CechComplex`/`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`. We record here the
655	structural ingredients that the exactness statement is phrased against: the
656	un-augmented {\v C}ech complex on `X` (`cechComplexOnX`), obtained from the {\v C}ech
657	nerve exactly as `relativeCechComplexOfNerve` does but with the *identity*
658	pushforward — i.e. directly, with no `f_*`. -/
659	
660	/-- **The (un-augmented) {\v C}ech cochain complex of `F` on `X`.** This is the
661	alternating-coface-map complex `C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of the underlying cosimplicial object
662	of the {\v C}ech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F` (its augmentation `F` is dropped). It is the
663	`f = 𝟙`/un-pushed analogue of `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`: the same coherence-free
664	plumbing without the relative pushforward `f_*`, so it is the complex whose
665	exactness (after re-adjoining the augmentation `F → C⁰`) is the content of
666	`cechAugmented_exact`. Project-local. -/
667	noncomputable def cechComplexOnX (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
668	    CochainComplex X.Modules ℕ :=
669	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex X.Modules).obj
670	    (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F))
671	
672	/-- **The augmentation point of the {\v C}ech nerve is `F`.** The augmentation
673	object `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left` of the {\v C}ech nerve is `(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F` (the
674	push–pull functor applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`), canonically
675	isomorphic to `F` via the unitors `pushforwardId`/`pullbackId`. Project-local. -/
676	noncomputable def cechNervePointIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
677	    (CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ≅ F :=
678	  (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X)).obj F) ≪≫
679	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X).app F
680	
681	/-- **The augmentation map `ε : F → C⁰` of the {\v C}ech nerve.** Built from the
682	augmentation natural transformation of the augmented cosimplicial object
683	`CechNerve 𝒰 F` (the map `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ⟶ C⁰`), pre-composed with the unitor
684	`cechNervePointIso` identifying the augmentation point with `F`. This is the map
685	prepended to `cechComplexOnX` to form the augmented {\v C}ech complex. Project-local. -/
686	noncomputable def cechAugmentation (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
687	    F ⟶ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).X 0 :=
688	  (cechNervePointIso 𝒰 F).inv ≫ (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0)
689	
690	/-- **Augmentation kills the first alternating-coface differential** (abstract form).
691	For any augmented cosimplicial object `N` in a preadditive category, the augmentation
692	map `N.hom.app [0] : N.left ⟶ (drop N).obj [0]` followed by the degree-0 alternating
693	coface differential `objD (drop N) 0 = δ⁰ - δ¹` is zero. This is the cosimplicial
694	augmentation identity (`ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹`, from naturality of `N.hom` against the two
695	coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`); stated abstractly so the proof does not unfold any concrete
696	(heavily whiskered) nerve. Project-local. -/
697	private lemma augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero
698	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C] (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented C) :
699	    N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) ≫
700	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
701	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N) 0 = 0 := by
702	  -- Ascribe the augmentation's codomain to the `𝟭`-free form `N.right.obj ⦋0⦌`; this is
703	  -- the `Comma`'s right functor being `𝟭`, which otherwise pins the composition's middle
704	  -- object to `(𝟭).obj N.right` and blocks every additive distribution lemma's instance.
705	  have hnat : ∀ i : Fin 2,
706	      (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
707	        N.right.δ i = N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 1) := by
708	    intro i
709	    have h := N.hom.naturality (SimplexCategory.δ i)
710	    simpa using h.symm
711	  show (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
712	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD N.right 0 = 0
713	  simp only [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD]
714	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_two]
715	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
716	  -- `erw` (defeq-matching) is needed: the `Comma`'s right functor `𝟭` pins the
717	  -- composition's middle object to `(𝟭).obj N.right`, so `comp_add`/`comp_neg`'s
718	  -- instances only match up to reducible defeq, not syntactically.
719	  erw [Preadditive.comp_add, Preadditive.comp_neg, hnat 0, hnat 1, add_neg_cancel]
720	
721	/-- **The augmentation composes to zero with `d⁰`.** The {\v C}ech augmentation
722	`ε : F → C⁰` followed by the first {\v C}ech differential `d⁰ : C⁰ → C¹` is zero,
723	the cochain-complex condition needed to prepend `ε` to `cechComplexOnX`. It is the
724	cosimplicial augmentation identity: `ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹` (naturality of the
725	augmentation natural transformation against the two coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`), so the
726	alternating sum `ε ≫ (δ⁰ - δ¹)` vanishes. Project-local. -/
727	lemma cechAugmentation_comp_d (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
728	    cechAugmentation 𝒰 F ≫ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 = 0 := by
729	  rw [cechAugmentation, Category.assoc]
730	  have hd : (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 =
731	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
732	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) 0 := rfl
733	  rw [hd]
734	  erw [augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero (CechNerve 𝒰 F)]
735	  exact Limits.comp_zero
736	
737	/-- **The augmented {\v C}ech complex on `X`** (Stacks
738	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`). The cochain complex
739	`0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` obtained from the un-augmented {\v C}ech complex `cechComplexOnX`
740	by prepending the augmentation `ε : F → C⁰` (`cechAugmentation`) in degree `0` — so
741	`(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).X 0 = F` and `(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).X (p+1) = Cᵖ`. The
742	augmentation condition `ε ≫ d⁰ = 0` is `cechAugmentation_comp_d`. The lemma
743	`cech_augmented_resolution` (`cechAugmented_exact`) asserts this complex is exact —
744	i.e. the {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of `F` in `QCoh(X)`. Project-local. -/
745	noncomputable def cechAugmentedComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
746	    CochainComplex X.Modules ℕ :=
747	  (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).augment (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)
748	
749	/-- **The Čech complex computes the higher direct images** (Stacks 02KE;
750	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent` and
751	`lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application`).
752	
753	Let `f : X ⟶ S` be separated and quasi-compact, `F` a quasi-coherent
754	`O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X` (so, by separatedness,
755	every intersection is affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative Čech
756	complex compute the higher direct images: for every `i ≥ 0` there is a canonical
757	isomorphism of `O_S`-modules
758	```
759	  Hⁱ(Č•(𝒰, F)) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F.
760	```
761	In particular, over an affine base `S = Spec A`, taking global sections gives
762	`Hⁱ(X, F) = Čⁱ(𝒰, F) = H⁰(S, Rⁱ f_* F)` as `A`-modules.
763	
764	We state the isomorphism as `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` and compare against the
765	derived-functor higher direct image `higherDirectImage` wherever the latter is
766	defined (`HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules`).
767	
768	Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the augmented Čech complex is a termwise
769	`f_*`-acyclic resolution of `F` (each term is a product of pushforwards over
770	affine intersections, acyclic by `CechAcyclic.affine` via the P3b bridge), so
771	the P4 acyclic-resolution lemma (`rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`) gives
772	`Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`. -/
773	theorem cech_computes_higherDirectImage [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
774	    (f : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f] (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
775	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ) :
776	    Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F) := by
777	  -- Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the Čech complex is a termwise acyclic
778	  -- resolution of `F` (by `CechAcyclic.affine`), so `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`
779	  -- gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`.
780	  sorry
781	
782	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	# Analogy: how to prove a complex in `X.Modules` is exact (Čech resolution)
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	stalkwise
8	
9	## Iteration
10	052
11	
12	## Question
13	What is the Mathlib-aligned way to prove `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is exact (the Čech
14	nerve is a resolution of a quasi-coherent `F`) in `X.Modules = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf`?
15	Stalk-reflection (forget to abelian sheaves → stalkwise) vs a sections-on-affines / basis detour.
16	Mathlib has **no** `SheafOfModules.stalk` functor and **no** "complex exact iff stalkwise exact".
17	
18	## Project artifact(s)
19	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean:745` — `cechAugmentedComplex` (frozen-area; the complex whose exactness is needed).
20	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean:773` — `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (FROZEN; Route A needs the resolution = exactness).
21	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean:1848` — `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` (section homology = 0 over `D(f)`, p ≥ 1).
22	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean:1868` — `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway` (same for a subcover of a proper `D(f)`).
23	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean:112` — `PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify` (**the keystone bridge**: module-sheaf homology = sheafify of presheaf homology).
24	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/AffineSerreVanishing.lean:119/151/233/373` — `toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`, `toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms`, `affine_surj_of_vanishing`, `affineCoverSystem` (the already-built `toSheaf`→AddCommGrp local-surjectivity + basis cover system).
25	
26	## Decisions identified
27	
28	### Decision A: the reflection backbone — `toSheaf` faithful, NOT a bespoke `SheafOfModules.stalk`
29	
30	- **Mathlib idiom**: `CategoryTheory.Functor.reflects_exact_of_faithful`
31	  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Exact`):
32	  for abelian `C, D` and `F : C ⥤ D` with `[F.PreservesZeroMorphisms] [F.Faithful]`,
33	  `(S.map F).Exact → S.Exact` for any `S : ShortComplex C`. **Only faithfulness + zero-preservation
34	  are required** — no need for `F` to be exact. `SheafOfModules.toSheaf R` is `Faithful`
35	  (`SheafOfModules.instFaithfulSheafAddCommGrpCatToSheaf`) and `Additive`
36	  (`SheafOfModules.instAdditiveSheafAddCommGrpCatToSheaf` ⟹ `PreservesZeroMorphisms`). It is also
37	  `ReflectsIsomorphisms` and `ReflectsFiniteLimits`
38	  (`PresheafOfModules.instReflectsIsomorphismsSheafOfModulesSheafAddCommGrpCatToSheaf`,
39	  `…instReflectsFiniteLimits…`). So `toSheaf` reflects exactness, isos, and zero objects for free.
40	- **Why Mathlib's shape works**: `toSheaf` lands in `TopCat.Sheaf AddCommGrp`, a *fixed* concrete
41	  abelian category — the varying-stalk-ring obstruction of `X.Modules` evaporates the moment you
42	  forget the module structure. You never construct a `SheafOfModules` stalk functor.
43	- **Project's path**: directive's sketch step (1) is correct and already partly used
44	  (`affine_surj_of_vanishing` forgets via `toSheaf`). The project's `toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`
45	  is NOT needed for the *reflection* (it was built for the epi/local-surjectivity argument).
46	- **Gap**: identical to Mathlib idiom.
47	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB — reflect through faithful `toSheaf`; do **not** build a
48	  `SheafOfModules.stalk` functor.
49	
50	### Decision B: how to discharge exactness of the *underlying* complex — sections/sheafification, NOT stalks
51	
52	- **Mathlib idiom (recommended)**: a sheaf-complex's homology is the **sheafification of the
53	  presheaf (section-level) homology**, and sheafification annihilates a presheaf that is **zero on a
54	  basis**. The two ingredients:
55	  1. `PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify` (PROJECT, `HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean:112`):
56	     `K.homology i ≅ (sheafification α).obj ((forget K).homology i)`. Built from Mathlib's
57	     `Functor.mapHomologyIso'` + sheafification-is-exact. **This already does the reflection in the
58	     module category** — no `toSheaf`/stalk needed at this step.
59	  2. The W-equivalence / locally-bijective theory (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.LocallyBijective`,
60	     `…/LocallySurjective`): `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso`,
61	     `Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_presheafToSheaf_map_iff`,
62	     `GrothendieckTopology.WEqualsLocallyBijective` (instance available for `AddCommGrp` via
63	     `instWEqualsLocallyBijectiveOfHasWeakSheafify…`). A presheaf `P` whose sections vanish on a
64	     basis makes `0 ⟶ P` locally injective (trivially) and locally surjective (P locally zero), i.e.
65	     a `J.W` (W-equivalence), so `presheafToSheaf.map (0 ⟶ P)` is iso ⟹ `sheafify P ≅ sheafify 0 = 0`.
66	  3. The basis vanishing is **already proved**: `sectionCech_affine_vanishing`
67	     (+ `…_of_localizationAway`) gives presheaf-homology = 0 over every distinguished open `D(f)`
68	     (degrees ≥ 1); the distinguished opens are a basis (`affineCoverSystem`, `standard_cover_cofinal`).
69	- **Why this beats stalks here**: "sections are not exact in general" is irrelevant — sheafification
70	  only needs *local* vanishing, and the section Čech homology genuinely *does* vanish on the affine
71	  basis. The whole computation reuses the project's existing localization homotopies
72	  (`combHomotopy`/`depHomotopy`) at the section level; nothing new about stalks.
73	- **The stalk alternative (NOT recommended)**: would require building (i) "`stalkFunctor AddCommGrp x`
74	  is exact / preserves homology" (Mathlib has only the AB5/filtered-colimit ingredients, no packaged
75	  instance), (ii) "abelian sheaf = 0 iff all stalks = 0" (Mathlib has `mono_iff_stalk_mono`,
76	  `isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso`, `section_ext` — but **not** an exactness-of-complex criterion),
77	  and (iii) the explicit stalk contracting homotopy of the Čech complex. (i)+(ii) are a genuine
78	  gap-fill; (iii) is the same homotopy as the section one, only re-derived via colimit. Strictly more
79	  to build, with no reuse advantage.
80	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost if the prover takes the stalk route (re-derives infrastructure the
81	  sheafification route already has); aligned if it takes the sections route.
82	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (sections/sheafification detour) — and AVOID the stalk route as a
83	  NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL it doesn't have to incur.
84	
85	## Recommendation
86	
87	Prove `cechAugmented_exact` via **homology-sheaf-is-sheafify-of-presheaf-homology, zero on the affine
88	basis** — never build a `SheafOfModules` stalk functor and never fill the "exact iff stalkwise" gap.
89	
90	Decomposition for a `mathlib-build` prover (rough LOC, lower-risk first):
91	
92	1. **`cechAugmented_exact`** (the target): `∀ i, IsZero ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).homology i)`
93	   ⟹ exact/`HomologicalComplex.Exact` (or the "is a resolution" packaging the frozen target consumes).
94	   Use `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology` family. ~30–50 LOC.
95	
96	2. **Sheafification kills basis-locally-zero homology**: from
97	   `homologyIsoSheafify`, `K.homology i ≅ sheafify((forget K).homology i)`. Show
98	   `0 ⟶ (forget K).homology i` is a `J.W` because each section over `D(f)` is `0`
99	   (locally surjective via the distinguished-open cover; locally injective trivially); conclude
100	   `sheafify(...) ≅ 0`. Lean on `Sheaf.isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso`,
101	   `isLocallySurjective_presheafToSheaf_map_iff`, the `WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrp` instance,
102	   and the project's `affineCoverSystem`/`standard_cover_cofinal` for the covering. Reflect
103	   `IsZero` back to `X.Modules` with `toSheaf` faithful (`reflects_exact_of_faithful` family /
104	   `Faithful` reflects zero objects). ~80–120 LOC. **Highest-risk piece** (sheafification plumbing).
105	
106	3. **Presheaf-homology = section Čech homology** over each open: identify
107	   `((forget (cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F))).homology i` evaluated on `U` with the
108	   `sectionCechComplex`-style homology, so step 2's input is exactly `sectionCech_affine_vanishing`.
109	   This is `mapHomologicalComplex`-vs-concrete-section plumbing — same diamond-prone flavor flagged in
110	   `[[keystone-tile-reconciliation-not-rfl]]` / `[[rR-semiring-diamond-change-workaround]]`. ~80–150 LOC.
111	
112	4. **Augmentation node (degree 0)**: `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` covers degrees ≥ 1; the
113	   augmented complex also needs exactness at `F→C⁰` and at `C⁰` (`H⁰ = M_f`, the sheaf/equalizer
114	   condition) over `D(f)`. Build the `Function.Exact` for the first two nodes via
115	   `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (same engine as `combDifferential_exact`/`depDiff_exact`, applied to
116	   the augmentation row). ~60–100 LOC.
117	
118	Total ≈ 250–420 LOC. Mathlib backbone: `reflects_exact_of_faithful`
119	(`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Exact`); `Sheaf.isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso`,
120	`isLocallySurjective_presheafToSheaf_map_iff`, `GrothendieckTopology.WEqualsLocallyBijective`
121	(`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.LocallyBijective` / `…/LocallySurjective`); `Functor.mapHomologyIso'`.
122	Project backbone: `PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`, `sectionCech_affine_vanishing`,
123	`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`, `affineCoverSystem`, `standard_cover_cofinal`,
124	`toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms`.

7100	\begin{definition}
7101	[Augmented {\v C}ech complex]
7102	  \label{def:cech_augmented_complex}
7103	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmentedComplex}
7104	  \uses{def:cech_complex_on_X, def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
7105	  \textit{Project-local.}
7106	  The \emph{augmented {\v C}ech complex} is the cochain complex
7107	  \[
7108	    0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0 \to \mathcal{C}^1 \to \cdots
7109	  \]
7110	  in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) obtained by prepending the coefficient sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) in degree \(0\)
7111	  to the complex of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex_on_X} along the augmentation
7112	  \(\varepsilon\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation}, using the augmentation identity
7113	  \(\varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}. Concretely its degree-\(0\)
7114	  term is \(\mathcal{F}\) and its degree-\((p+1)\) term is \(\mathcal{C}^p\). This is the object whose
7115	  exactness is the content of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}.
7116	\end{definition}

7118	\begin{lemma}
7119	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
7120	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
7121	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
7122	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, def:cech_augmented_complex, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}
7123	  % NOTE: the augmented-complex object layer (cechComplexOnX, cechNervePointIso, cechAugmentation,
7124	  % cechAugmentation_comp_d, cechAugmentedComplex) is formalized in CechHigherDirectImage.lean.

7127	  % criterion for X.Modules not yet in the library; the sections-and-sheafification route of the

7129	  % definition block and a separate exactness lemma, adding a stalkwise-exactness sub-lemma if needed.
7130	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
7131	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof: exactness of the
7132	  % extended {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L68--82).
7133	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
7134	  % \begin{equation}
7135	  % \label{equation-extended}
7136	  % 0 \to
7137	  % M \to
7138	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
7139	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to

7141	  % \ldots
7142	  % \end{equation}
7143	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
7144	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
7145	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
7146	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
7147	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}."
7148	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
7149	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
7150	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
7151	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
7152	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
7153	  \[
7154	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
7155	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
7156	    \qquad
7157	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
7158	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
7159	  \]
7160	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
7161	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
7162	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
7163	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
7164	  The object underlying this statement --- the augmented complex
7165	  \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0 \to \mathcal{C}^1 \to \cdots\) of
7166	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmented_complex} together with its augmentation and the identity
7167	  \(\varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\) --- is already constructed; what remains is the
7168	  exactness (acyclicity) asserted here.
7169	\end{lemma}
7170	
7171	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
7172	% is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
7173	% Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}.
7174	% In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
7175	% at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
7176	% There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
7177	% Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. Note that
7178	% $M_{f_{i_{\text{fix}}}, \mathfrak p} = M_{\mathfrak p}$. ...
7179	% Let us define a homotopy ... by the rule
7180	% $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}$"
7181	% (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L80--110).
7182	\begin{proof}
7183	  \uses{def:cech_augmented_complex, lem:section_cech_homology_exact,
7184	    lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology,
7185	    lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}
7186	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
7187	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
7188	  We prove exactness of the augmented {\v C}ech complex of
7189	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmented_complex} in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) by showing that each homology
7190	  object \(\mathcal{H}^p\) (\(p \geq 1\), together with the augmentation node) is the zero object. The
7191	  argument works entirely with sections and sheafification, never with stalks.
7192	
7193	  \emph{Step 1: reflect through the forgetful functor to abelian sheaves.} The forgetful functor from
7194	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to sheaves of abelian groups is faithful and additive, hence
7195	  preserves zero morphisms; a faithful additive functor reflects the property of an object being zero.
7196	  It therefore suffices to prove that the image of each homology object \(\mathcal{H}^p\) under this
7197	  forgetful functor is zero in the category of abelian sheaves.
7198	
7199	  \emph{Step 2: the homology sheaf is the sheafification of the presheaf homology.} By the
7200	  sheafification-commutes-with-homology engine of
7201	  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}, the degree-\(p\) homology of the complex of
7202	  abelian sheaves is the sheafification of the degree-\(p\) homology of the underlying complex of
7203	  \emph{presheaves}. The presheaf homology evaluated at an open \(V\) is the homology of the section
7204	  complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})(V)\) --- the {\v C}ech cochain complex
7205	  of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the cover restricted to \(V\) --- so the presheaf in question is
7206	  \(V \mapsto \check{H}^p(V, \mathcal{F})\).
7207	
7208	  \emph{Step 3: the presheaf homology vanishes locally on the affine basis.} This presheaf is not zero
7209	  as a presheaf, but it vanishes on a basis. Over any basic affine \(D(g)\) small enough to sit inside
7210	  a single cover element \(U_i\), the affine tilde isomorphism
7211	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}) identifies \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(g)}\) with \(\widetilde{M}\),
7212	  and the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\widetilde{M}\) over the induced standard cover is exact in
7213	  positive degrees: this is precisely Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover} (whose
7214	  section-level exactness packaging is Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}). Since the basic
7215	  affines contained in the cover elements form a basis cofinal among all opens, the canonical map
7216	  \(0 \to (\text{presheaf homology})\) is a local isomorphism --- it is locally bijective. A map of
7217	  sheaves is an isomorphism if and only if it is locally bijective, and sheafification carries a locally
7218	  bijective map to an isomorphism; hence the sheafification of the presheaf homology is the zero sheaf.
7219	  Combined with Step 2 this gives \(\mathcal{H}^p = 0\) for all \(p \geq 1\).
7220	
7221	  \emph{Step 4: the augmentation node.} Separately, the augmentation \(\varepsilon : \mathcal{F} \to
7222	  \mathcal{C}^0\) is the kernel inclusion: the degree-\(0\) homology of the augmented complex vanishes
7223	  because over each basic affine the augmented section complex is exact at degree \(0\) (the same
7224	  spanning-family argument that gives exactness in positive degrees produces exactness at the
7225	  augmentation node), and sheafifying this local exactness as in Step 3 makes the augmentation node
7226	  homology zero. Together with Steps 1--3, every homology object of the augmented complex is zero, so
7227	  the complex is exact and the {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} is a resolution of
7228	  \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\).
7229	
7230	  \emph{Remark.} An alternative proof uses the stalk-at-prime criterion directly: the complex is
7231	  exact if and only if it is exact after localizing at each prime \(\mathfrak{p}\), and for each
7232	  \(\mathfrak{p}\) one fixes an index \(i_{\text{fix}}\) with \(f_{i_{\text{fix}}} \notin \mathfrak{p}\)
7233	  and defines a contracting homotopy of the localized complex by
7234	  \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\), as in the Stacks source proof above.
7235	  This approach is equivalent to the sections-and-sheafification argument of Steps 1--4.
7236	\end{proof}
7237	
7238	\begin{lemma}
7239	[Cohomology sheaf is the sheafification of the objectwise homology]
7240	  \label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
7241	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
7242	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
7243	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
7244	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
7245	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
7246	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
7247	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
7248	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
7249	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
7250	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
7251	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
7252	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
7253	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
7254	  \[
7255	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
7256	      \;\cong\;
7257	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
7258	  \]
7259	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
7260	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
7261	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
7262	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
7263	\end{lemma}
7264	
7265	\begin{proof}
7266	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
7267	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
7268	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
7269	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
7270	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
7271	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
7272	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
7273	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
7274	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
7275	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
7276	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
7277	  additive homology-preserving functor.
7278	\end{proof}
7279	
7280	\begin{lemma}
7281	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]
7282	  \label{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
7283	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology,
7284	    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex}
7285	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
7286	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01XJ, Lemma
7287	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
7288	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
7289	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
7290	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
7291	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
7292	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
7293	  % associated to the presheaves
7294	  % $$
7295	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
7296	  % $$
7297	  % with restriction mappings as in Equation (\ref{equation-restriction-mapping}).
7298	  % There is a similar statement for $R^if_*$ applied to a
7299	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
7300	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
7301	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
7302	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
7303	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has
7304	  enough injective objects, so that the derived pushforward \(R^k f_*\) is defined.
7305	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
7306	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and push it forward degreewise to obtain the cochain complex
7307	  of presheaves of modules \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) on \(S\). For every \(k \geq 0\)
7308	  the higher direct image \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is isomorphic to the
7309	  \emph{sheafification of the presheaf of objectwise homology} of this pushed complex,
7310	  \[
7311	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
7312	      \;\cong\;
7313	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
7314	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
7315	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
7316	  \]
7317	  the sheafification of the \(k\)-th presheaf-level homology of
7318	  \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\). As a closing remark, this gives the affine-local
7319	  vanishing criterion consumed downstream
7320	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} and
7321	  \ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}): since the sheafification of a presheaf
7322	  vanishes exactly when that presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens,
7323	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = 0\) iff \(H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr) = 0\)
7324	  for every \(V\) in a basis of the topology of \(S\); the affine opens form such a
7325	  basis, so it suffices to check the vanishing on affine \(V\).
7326	
7327	  The identification of the displayed presheaf homology with the absolute cohomology
7328	  of the preimage,
7329	  \[
7330	    H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
7331	      \;=\;
7332	    H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
7333	  \]
7334	  which uses that \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is again injective over
7335	  \(f^{-1}(V)\), is supplied at point of use; this lemma records the
7336	  resolution-internal sheafify-of-objectwise-homology form.
7337	\end{lemma}
7338	
7339	\begin{proof}
7340	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
7341	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
7342	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
7343	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of presheaves of modules
7344	  over \(S\). The comparison engine of
7345	  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology} now applies: taking homology in
7346	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level)
7347	  homology, because the sheafification/inclusion adjunction is exact (it preserves the
7348	  finite limits and colimits computing kernels and cokernels). Hence
7349	  \[
7350	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
7351	      \;\cong\;
7352	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
7353	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
7354	  \]
7355	  which is the stated isomorphism. Since the sheafification of a presheaf vanishes
7356	  exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens, this yields the
7357	  affine-local vanishing criterion.
7358	\end{proof}

7360	\begin{lemma}
7361	[Affine open immersions are $(\cdot)_*$-acyclic and degenerate the composition]
7362	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
7363	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}

7366	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct

7369	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
7370	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
7371	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
7372	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
7373	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
7374	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
7375	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
7376	  \to S\). Then:
7377	  \begin{enumerate}
7378	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
7379	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
7380	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
7381	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
7382	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
7383	      \(k \geq 0\),
7384	      \[
7385	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
7386	      \]
7387	  \end{enumerate}
7388	\end{lemma}

7390	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
7391	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
7392	% the sheaf
7393	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
7394	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
7395	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
7396	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
7397	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
7398	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$

7400	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
7401	\begin{proof}
7402	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}

7404	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the

7407	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description

10	/-!
11	# Presheaf description of the higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` (Stacks 01XJ)
12	
13	This file builds project-local infrastructure towards
14	`lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf`: the higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F` is the
15	sheafification of the presheaf `V ↦ Hⁱ(f⁻¹(V), F)`.
16	
17	The categorical heart of Stacks 01XJ is that **cohomology of a complex of sheaves
18	of modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level) homology**:
19	this holds because the sheafification functor of presheaves of modules is exact
20	(it preserves finite limits — `Mathlib`'s
21	`PresheafOfModules.instPreservesFiniteLimitsSheafification` — and, being a left
22	adjoint, finite colimits), hence preserves homology, and the counit of the
23	sheafification adjunction is an isomorphism on sheaves.
24	
25	This file isolates that engine (`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`) and feeds
26	it through the right-derived-functor computation
27	(`InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj`) to express `Rⁿ f_* G`, for a chosen
28	injective resolution of `G`, as the sheafification of the presheaf-level homology
29	of the pushed-forward resolution
30	(`AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology`). The
31	remaining step to the blueprint statement — identifying that presheaf-level
32	homology with the cohomology presheaf `V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` — is handed off (see
33	the `## Why I stopped` note in the task result).
34	
35	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`
36	(`lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf`, `def:higher_direct_image`).
37	-/
38	
39	universe v u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits
42	
43	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — homology of a complex under a functor -/
44	
45	namespace CategoryTheory.Functor
46	
47	/-- The homological-complex level of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso`: an additive,
48	homology-preserving functor `F` commutes with taking homology of a complex,
49	`((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).homology i ≅ F.obj (K.homology i)`.
50	
51	Project-local: `Mathlib` provides this isomorphism only at the `ShortComplex`
52	level (`ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso`); we lift it degreewise to
53	`HomologicalComplex` (the two `homology` objects are definitionally the homology
54	of the relevant short complexes, so the lift is the `ShortComplex` isomorphism
55	applied to `K.sc i`). -/
56	noncomputable def mapHomologyIso'
57	    {A B : Type*} [Category A] [Category B] [Abelian A] [Abelian B]
58	    (F : A ⥤ B) [F.Additive] [F.PreservesHomology]
59	    {ι : Type*} (c : ComplexShape ι) (K : HomologicalComplex A c) (i : ι) :
60	    ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).homology i ≅ F.obj (K.homology i) :=
61	  (K.sc i).mapHomologyIso F
62	
63	end CategoryTheory.Functor

65	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheaf homology is sheafified presheaf homology -/
66	
67	namespace PresheafOfModules
68	
69	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
70	  {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{v}} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{v}} (α : R₀ ⟶ R.obj)
71	  [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
72	  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
73	  {ιc : Type} (cc : ComplexShape ιc)
74	
75	/-- The sheafification functor of presheaves of modules is additive (it preserves
76	binary products, being a left adjoint that preserves finite limits).
77	
78	Project-local: needed so that `sheafification α` can be applied to homological
79	complexes via `mapHomologicalComplex`. -/
80	noncomputable instance sheafificationAdditive :
81	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).Additive :=
82	  Functor.additive_of_preserves_binary_products _
83	
84	/-- The counit of the sheafification adjunction, applied levelwise to a complex of
85	sheaves of modules, is an isomorphism of complexes
86	`sheafify (forget K) ≅ K`. This is the sheaf-side input to
87	`homologyIsoSheafify`: a sheaf complex is recovered (up to canonical iso) by
88	sheafifying its underlying presheaf complex.
89	
90	Project-local: assembled from `Mathlib`'s `IsIso (sheafificationAdjunction α).counit`
91	via `isoOfComponents`. -/
92	noncomputable def counitComplexIso (K : HomologicalComplex (SheafOfModules.{v} R) cc) :
93	    ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj
94	        (((SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex
95	          cc).obj K) ≅ K :=
96	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents
97	    (fun n => (asIso (sheafificationAdjunction α).counit).app (K.X n))
98	    (fun n m _ => ((sheafificationAdjunction α).counit.naturality (K.d n m)).symm)
99	
100	/-- **Engine for Stacks 01XJ.** Homology of a complex of sheaves of modules is the
101	sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of its underlying
102	presheaf complex:
103	`K.homology i ≅ (sheafification α).obj ((forget K).homology i)`.
104	
105	The proof transports homology across the counit isomorphism `sheafify (forget K) ≅ K`
106	of `counitComplexIso` and then uses that `sheafification α` preserves homology
107	(it is exact: preserves finite limits and, as a left adjoint, finite colimits),
108	via `Functor.mapHomologyIso'`.
109	
110	Project-local: `Mathlib` has the exactness ingredients but not this packaged
111	"cohomology sheaf = sheafify of objectwise cohomology" comparison. -/
112	noncomputable def homologyIsoSheafify (K : HomologicalComplex (SheafOfModules.{v} R) cc)
113	    (i : ιc) :
114	    K.homology i ≅ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).obj
115	      ((((SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex
116	        cc).obj K).homology i) :=
117	  (HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor _ cc i).mapIso (counitComplexIso α cc K).symm ≪≫
118	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).mapHomologyIso' cc
119	      (((SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex
120	        cc).obj K) i
121	
122	end PresheafOfModules
123	
124	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Rⁿ f_*` as a sheafified presheaf homology -/
125	
126	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
127	
128	variable {S X : Scheme.{u}}
129	
130	/-- The underlying presheaf-of-modules complex of the pushed-forward injective
131	resolution `f_* I^•`, whose `n`-th objectwise homology is the cohomology presheaf
132	`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` (with the implicit choice of injective resolution `I`).
133	
134	Project-local: the presheaf whose sheafification is `Rⁿ f_* G`. -/
135	noncomputable def pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex
136	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] (f : X ⟶ S) {G : X.Modules}
137	    (I : InjectiveResolution G) :
138	    HomologicalComplex (PresheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf.obj) (ComplexShape.up ℕ) :=
139	  ((SheafOfModules.forget S.ringCatSheaf ⋙
140	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapHomologicalComplex
141	        (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
142	    (((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj I.cocomplex)
143	
144	/-- **Stacks 01XJ, resolution form.** For any chosen injective resolution
145	`I : InjectiveResolution G`, the `n`-th higher direct image `Rⁿ f_* G` is the
146	sheafification of the presheaf-level homology of the pushed-forward resolution
147	`f_* I^•`. Concretely, `Rⁿ f_* G ≅ sheafify(V ↦ Hⁿ((f_* I^•)(V)))`, and the
148	presheaf `V ↦ Hⁿ((f_* I^•)(V)) = Hⁿ(I^•(f⁻¹ V))` is the cohomology presheaf
149	`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` of the blueprint statement.
150	
151	The proof composes `InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj` (computing the right
152	derived functor `Rⁿ f_* = (f_*).rightDerived n` via the resolution) with the
153	sheafification engine `PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`.
154	
155	Project-local: the resolution-dependent form of `lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf`;
156	the identification of the displayed presheaf with the absolute cohomology presheaf
157	`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` is the remaining hand-off step. -/
158	noncomputable def higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology
159	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] (f : X ⟶ S) (n : ℕ) {G : X.Modules}
160	    (I : InjectiveResolution G) :
161	    higherDirectImage f n G ≅
162	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
163	        ((pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex f I).homology n) :=
164	  I.isoRightDerivedObj (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f) n ≪≫
165	    PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj) (ComplexShape.up ℕ)
166	      (((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
167	        I.cocomplex) n
168	
169	end AlgebraicGeometry

I have enough API knowledge. The transport lemma is pure Mathlib and reusable. Let me test it standalone with `lean_run_code` before committing to the file.

input.code:
import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory Limits

example {C : Type*} [Category C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C) {A : Type*} [Category A]
    [HasWeakSheafify J A] {P Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P ⟶ Q) (hf : J.W f)
    (hQ : IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj Q)) :
    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj P) := by
  have : IsIso ((presheafToSheaf J A).map f) := (J.W_iff f).1 hf
  exact hQ.of_iso (asIso ((presheafToSheaf J A).map f))

content: {"success":true,"diagnostics":[]}

input.code:
import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory Limits

-- (a) sheafification sends the zero presheaf to a zero object
example {C : Type*} [Category C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C) {A : Type*} [Category A]
    [Preadditive A] [HasZeroObject A] [HasWeakSheafify J A] :
    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj 0) := by
  have : (presheafToSheaf J A).Additive := by infer_instance
  exact (presheafToSheaf J A).map_isZero (isZero_zero _)

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory Limits

-- (2) additive sheafification preserves zero objects (no `0` literal)
example {C : Type*} [Category C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C) {A : Type*} [Category A]
    [Abelian A] [HasSheafify J A] {P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (hP : IsZero P) :
    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj P) := by
  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J A).Additive := presheafToSheaf_additive
  exact (presheafToSheaf J A).map_isZero hP

-- (3) combined: W-equivalent to a zero presheaf ⟹ sheafify is zero
example {C : Type*} [Category C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C) {A : Type*} [Category A]
    [Abelian A] [HasSheafify J A] {P Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P ⟶ Q) (hf : J.W f)
    (hQ : IsZero Q) : IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj P) := by
  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J A).Additive := presheafToSheaf_additive
  have hQs : IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj Q) := (presheafToSheaf J A).map_isZero hQ
  have : IsIso ((presheafToSheaf J A).map f) := (J.W_iff f).1 hf
  exact hQs.of_iso (asIso ((presheafToSheaf J A).map f))

-- (4) locally bijective entry
example {C : Type*} [Category C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C) {A : Type*} [Category A]
    {FA : A → A → Type*} {CA : A → Type*} [(X Y : A) → FunLike (FA X Y) (CA X) (CA Y)]
    [ConcreteCategory A FA] [Abelian A] [HasSheafify J A] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective A]
    {P Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P ⟶ Q) [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f]
    [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f] (hQ : IsZero Q) :
    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj P) := by
  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J A).Additive := presheafToSheaf_additive
  have hQs : IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj Q) := (presheafToSheaf J A).map_isZero hQ
  have : IsIso ((presheafToSheaf J A).map f) := (J.W_iff f).1 (J.W_of_isLocallyBijective f)
  exact hQs.of_iso (asIso ((presheafToSheaf J A).map f))

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification annihilates a locally-zero presheaf

These three site-theoretic lemmas are the reusable **Step 2** of the
sections/sheafification route to {\v C}ech-resolution exactness
(`lem:cech_augmented_resolution`): the homology sheaf of a complex of sheaves of
modules is the sheafification of the *presheaf* homology
(`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`), and that sheafification is the zero
object whenever the presheaf homology is *locally zero* — i.e. it receives a local
isomorphism (`J.W`-equivalence) from, or is locally bijective onto, a presheaf that
is objectwise zero.

They are stated for a general Grothendieck topology and an arbitrary abelian
(concrete) target, depend only on Mathlib, and live in this — the most upstream
`Cohomology` file — so that the downstream exactness argument (whose section-level
vanishing input `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` and the bridge
`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify` both reside in files that *import* this one)
can consume them without an import cycle. -/

namespace CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology

variable {C : Type*} [Category C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)

/-- **Sheafification transports the zero-object property across a `J.W`-equivalence.**
If `f : P ⟶ Q` is a local isomorphism (`J.W f`) and the sheafification of `Q` is a
zero object, then so is the sheafification of `P`. The local isomorphism becomes an
isomorphism after sheafification (`GrothendieckTopology.W_iff`), and zero objects are
stable under isomorphism. Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W {A : Type*} [Category A] [HasWeakSheafify J A]
    {P Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P ⟶ Q) (hf : J.W f)
    (hQ : Limits.IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj Q)) :
    Limits.IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj P) := by
  have : IsIso ((presheafToSheaf J A).map f) := (J.W_iff f).1 hf
  exact hQ.of_iso (asIso ((presheafToSheaf J A).map f))

/-- **Sheafification of a presheaf locally isomorphic to a zero presheaf is zero.**
If `f : P ⟶ Q` is a `J.W`-equivalence and `Q` is *objectwise* a zero object, then the
sheafification of `P` is the zero object. (Sheafification is additive over an abelian
target — `presheafToSheaf_additive` — so it carries the zero presheaf `Q` to a zero
sheaf; combine with `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W`.) This is the form the
{\v C}ech-resolution argument applies to the presheaf homology `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V, F)`, which is
locally zero on the affine basis. Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero {A : Type*} [Category A] [Abelian A]
    [HasSheafify J A] {P Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P ⟶ Q) (hf : J.W f) (hQ : Limits.IsZero Q) :
    Limits.IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj P) := by
  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J A).Additive := presheafToSheaf_additive
  exact isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W J f hf ((presheafToSheaf J A).map_isZero hQ)

/-- **Sheafification of a presheaf locally bijective onto a zero presheaf is zero.**
Concrete-target form of `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero`: when the topology
satisfies `WEqualsLocallyBijective` (e.g. `AddCommGrpCat`), a map `f : P ⟶ Q` that is
locally injective and locally surjective is a `J.W`-equivalence
(`GrothendieckTopology.W_of_isLocallyBijective`); if moreover `Q` is objectwise zero,
the sheafification of `P` vanishes. The downstream consumer obtains the local
injectivity from the basis-vanishing of the section {\v C}ech homology and the local
surjectivity for free (the target is a zero presheaf). Project-local Mathlib
supplement (site theory). -/
lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective {A : Type*} [Category A]
    {FA : A → A → Type*} {CA : A → Type*} [(X Y : A) → FunLike (FA X Y) (CA X) (CA Y)]
    [ConcreteCategory A FA] [Abelian A] [HasSheafify J A] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective A]
    {P Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P ⟶ Q) [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f]
    [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f] (hQ : Limits.IsZero Q) :
    Limits.IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj P) :=
  isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero J f (J.W_of_isLocallyBijective f) hQ

end CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (3, all axiom-clean):**
  `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W`,
  `…isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero`,
  `…isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective` (new section at EOF, lines ~784–847).
- **Declarations blocked (1):** `cechAugmented_exact` — **NOT added** (a `sorry`-free version is
  impossible in this file; see the hard structural blocker below). Left absent per the
  no-sorry invariant.
- **Sorry count across this file: 1 → 1** (only the pre-existing FROZEN
  `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` at line 773; untouched). Build GREEN.

## HARD STRUCTURAL BLOCKER (the headline — needs a planner/routing decision)

`cechAugmented_exact` **cannot be proved in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`** via the prescribed
sections/sheafification route, because **every prescribed ingredient lives in a file that
transitively imports this one** — importing any of them back is an import cycle:

| Ingredient (from PROGRESS Lane B / analogist) | Lives in | Imports CechHigherDirectImage? |
|---|---|---|
| `PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify` (the keystone bridge) | `HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean` | **YES (direct)** |
| `sectionCech_affine_vanishing`, `…_of_localizationAway` (the math heart) | `CechAcyclic.lean` | **YES (direct)** |
| `sectionCechComplex` | `PresheafCech.lean` | **YES (direct)** |
| `affineCoverSystem`, `standard_cover_cofinal` | `AffineSerreVanishing.lean` | YES (transitive) |
| `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` | `QcohTildeSections.lean` | YES (transitive) |

`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` imports only `HigherDirectImage` (+ `Mathlib`); it is the **most
upstream** Cohomology file. The deep section-vanishing machinery (≈1800 lines of CechAcyclic) is
not re-derivable here. This is NOT a Mathlib gap and NOT a "try harder" situation — it is a file
placement issue.

**Recommended fix (planner): relocate the exactness theorem.** Create / assign
`cechAugmented_exact` in a **new downstream file** (or an existing one such as
`AffineSerreVanishing.lean`, which already transitively imports CechAcyclic + everything else)
that imports `CechAcyclic`, `HigherDirectImagePresheaf`, `AffineSerreVanishing`,
`QcohTildeSections`. The augmented-complex object layer (`cechAugmentedComplex` et al.) stays
here and is visible downstream. The `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}` blueprint pin
just points at the new location.
Alternative (heavier): move `homologyIsoSheafify` + section-vanishing infra upstream into this
file — not recommended (drags ~2k LOC of CechAcyclic upward).

## What I built instead (the genuinely upstream-buildable Step-2 core)

Step 2 of the route ("sheafification annihilates a locally-zero presheaf homology") is **pure
Mathlib site theory** and needs nothing downstream, so it correctly belongs in this upstream file
where all downstream consumers can import it. I landed the three reusable lemmas:

### `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W` (line ~810) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** `J.W f → IsZero (presheafToSheaf.obj Q) → IsZero (presheafToSheaf.obj P)`.
- **Approach:** `GrothendieckTopology.W_iff` makes `presheafToSheaf.map f` an iso; `IsZero.of_iso`.

### `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero` (line ~826) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** for abelian target with `HasSheafify`, `J.W f → IsZero Q (objectwise) → IsZero
  (presheafToSheaf.obj P)`.
- **Approach:** `presheafToSheaf_additive` ⇒ sheafification carries the zero presheaf to a zero
  sheaf (`Functor.map_isZero`); chain through the transport lemma above. This is the form the
  downstream argument applies to the presheaf homology `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V,F)`.

### `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective` (line ~843) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** concrete abelian target with `WEqualsLocallyBijective` (e.g. `AddCommGrpCat`):
  `[IsLocallyInjective J f] [IsLocallySurjective J f] → IsZero Q → IsZero (presheafToSheaf.obj P)`.
- **Approach:** `GrothendieckTopology.W_of_isLocallyBijective` then the previous lemma. This is the
  natural consumer entry: the downstream argument gets local injectivity from basis-vanishing of
  the section Čech homology, and local surjectivity is free (target is a zero presheaf).

All verified: `#print axioms` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` for each.

## cechAugmented_exact (NOT added)
- **Approach 1 (prescribed sections/sheafification):** blocked — every ingredient
  (`homologyIsoSheafify`, `sectionCech_affine_vanishing`, …) is downstream ⇒ import cycle. Not a
  math failure; a file-placement failure. See blocker table above.
- **Approach 2 (stalk route):** already a documented dead end (no `SheafOfModules.stalk`,
  no "exact iff stalkwise" in Mathlib); also would still need the downstream section homotopies.
- **Next bridge for the relocated theorem (the one remaining gap after my lemmas):** connect the
  *module*-sheafification `(PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).obj P` (what `homologyIsoSheafify`
  produces) to my `presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat` lemmas, by reflecting `IsZero` through the
  faithful additive `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` and matching `toSheaf ∘ sheafification` with
  `presheafToSheaf ∘ forget` (the `sheafificationCompToSheaf` square; cf.
  `analogies/tosheaf-epi.md`). I did not attempt this here: it is module-specific, diamond-prone,
  and — crucially — the consumer that needs it does not exist in any importable file yet, so it
  would be unconsumed dead code until the relocation above happens. It should be built in the
  relocated file next to its single use, where `toSheaf`/`forget`/`homologyIsoSheafify` are in
  scope.

## Needs blueprint entry
Three new **non-private** declarations (pure-Mathlib site-theory supplement; no blueprint block
yet — will show up under `archon dag-query unmatched`). Suggest a single small block (they form
one coherent Step-2 unit), e.g. under `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`'s proof `\uses`, or a new
`lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero` referenced by Step 2/3:

- `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W`
  — file `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`. Relies on: `GrothendieckTopology.W_iff`,
  `Limits.IsZero.of_iso`, `asIso`.
- `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero`
  — same file. Relies on: `presheafToSheaf_additive`, `Functor.map_isZero`, and the lemma above.
- `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective`
  — same file. Relies on: `GrothendieckTopology.W_of_isLocallyBijective` and the lemma above.

These directly formalize the blueprint Step-2 claim "sheafification carries a locally bijective
map to an isomorphism; hence the sheafification of the (locally-zero) presheaf homology is the
zero sheaf" in `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`.

## Why I stopped
- **Partial progress (real):** 3 axiom-clean declarations added (lines ~810, ~826, ~843), the
  pure-Mathlib Step-2 core of the route, correctly placed in the most-upstream file so every
  downstream consumer can import them.
- **Hard blocker on the assigned target:** `cechAugmented_exact` is structurally impossible to
  prove in this file (import cycle — all ingredients are downstream). This is a routing decision
  only the planner can make: relocate the theorem to a downstream file (recommended:
  `AffineSerreVanishing.lean` or a new file importing CechAcyclic + HigherDirectImagePresheaf).
  I did not insert a sorry, did not add a hypothesis-weakened stand-in under the pinned name, and
  left the declaration absent per the invariant.
- **No external-LLM key** (per PROGRESS standing note) — informal agent unavailable; used LSP +
  Mathlib search instead.
- **Approaches identified but deliberately not attempted (with reason):** the module-sheafification
  → `presheafToSheaf` reflection bridge (Approach-2-next above) — unconsumable here (consumer is
  downstream and absent) and diamond-prone; belongs in the relocated file. Flagged precisely for
  next iter rather than built as dead code.

Proving the theorem here would require importing those back = an import cycle. I did **not** insert a `sorry`, did not add a weakened stand-in under the pinned name, and left the declaration absent per the invariant.

**What I built instead — 3 axiom-clean, pure-Mathlib lemmas (Step 2 of the route):** sheafification annihilates a locally-zero / W-trivial presheaf. Placed in this most-upstream file so every downstream consumer can import them without a cycle:

- `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective` — locally-bijective onto a zero presheaf ⇒ sheafify is zero

All verified `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. File builds GREEN (only the pre-existing frozen `sorry` at line 773 remains; sorry count 1 → 1).
